title: Durable Due Scheduling, Clock Injection, Timers, Backoff, and Pause · Changed-Schema Lab

objectives: Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher guidance, pause cause, and schedule reason outside process timers.
Inject clock and sleep ports, distinguish elapsed from civil time, define boundary equality, and test skew and jumps.

Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 4741 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove durable due state, injected clocks, non-overlapping timers, backoff, pause, and handle cleanup; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, migrations, lockfile, package, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only.
